# Docs: private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model

## Why

The snapshot model adds an operational requirement the old "read the local checkout" model
did not have — the server must be able to `fetch` from origin, which for private repos means
credentials. Operators and agents need clear guidance and clear failures.

## What

- Update the MCP-server setup docs to state the PRIVATE-REPO FETCH CREDENTIAL requirement:
  the server's clone needs a credential helper / deploy key / token to fetch arbitrary refs.
  Target docs (named so each AC is a deterministic, grep-able check): the MCP-server setup
  section of `README.md` and `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` (and any dedicated MCP setup doc).
- Document the descriptive credential-failure behavior (message + remedy) and that attested
  mode fails closed while local mode still runs.
- Document the `ref` + `source` semantics: attested (default, pinned SHA, SIGNS, records
  `verified_at_sha`) vs local (in-place checkout, NEVER signs); the `origin/main` default.
- Document the HMAC trust model + limits (integrity + intra-domain authenticity only; no
  non-repudiation / public verifiability / transparency) and the in-toto Statement shape.
- Document `REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR`, the disk-cap behavior, and the EFS/NFS flock caveat (cross-ref
  alto-fruit-punch).

## Scope

- In: setup-doc credential section, error-behavior docs, ref/source + signing semantics, HMAC
  trust-model doc, the new env/knobs — each pinned to a named doc file.
- Out: code (S1-S5).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `README.md` (MCP-setup section) and `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` state the private-repo
      fetch-credential requirement and how to supply it (grep-able for the credential section).
- [ ] The descriptive missing-credential error (message + remedy) and the fail-closed (attested)
      vs local-still-runs behavior are documented in a named file.
- [ ] `ref`/`source` semantics, the `origin/main` default, AND the signs-vs-never-signs +
      `verified_at_sha` recording are documented for CLI and MCP (named file).
- [ ] The HMAC trust model + limits and the in-toto Statement shape are documented (named file).
- [ ] `REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR`, disk-cap behavior, and the EFS/NFS flock caveat are documented
      (xref alto-fruit-punch), each in a named file.
